2024-10-18.thesis: Amazon signs nuclear power deal, boosting uranium fuel demand
2024-10-18.exit_case: Amazon's nuclear deal was publicly signed ~Oct 16 — the discrete announcement event has already occurred and stocks have already surged on it.
2024-10-18.assessment: Week 1. Amazon signed a nuclear power deal (~Oct 16), becoming the latest in a Big Tech wave (Microsoft/TMC, Google/Kairos, Amazon) to secure long-term nuclear power — creating a structural uranium demand tailwind that goes well beyond the single signing event. The thesis is not the announcement itself but the cascading demand it triggers: multi-year fuel contracting, enrichment agreements, reactor construction timelines, and likely further tech-company nuclear deals. These are concrete pending milestones that keep catalyst_resolved=false. CCJ is the cleanest pure-play (named explicitly in IBD as 'Stock of the Day,' described as 'exploding'). URA captures the basket with less single-name risk. UUUU and DNN offer higher-beta US-focused uranium production exposure. OKLO is a different mechanism (SMR developer, not uranium miner) but rides the same wave and is in the known vehicle list. KEY CAUTION: mainstream saturation is heavy from day 1 — Yahoo Finance, Morningstar, Benzinga, Investors.com, Invezz all publishing in the same week; OKLO already +100% this month; retail 'is now a good time?' articles already appearing. The early-entry edge is partially spent. The structural demand story is real and durable, but conviction starts at 6 rather than higher precisely because this arrived in the press fully formed and crowded.
2024-10-18.exit_advice: Hold through quiet stretches — this is a structural, multi-year nuclear buildout with many concrete milestones still ahead (fuel contracting cycles, enrichment agreements, construction starts, additional Big Tech deals). Exit only on a genuine reversal: uranium spot price collapse, regulatory block on new reactors, or Big Tech visibly pulling back from nuclear commitments.

2024-10-25.thesis: Amazon signs nuclear power deal, boosting uranium fuel demand
2024-10-25.exit_case: All three Big Tech nuclear deals signed; no new signing scheduled; retail frenzy confirms crowd fully arrived.
2024-10-25.assessment: Week 2. Third Exit condition is met. (a) Arc complete: the initial shock was a wave of Big Tech nuclear signings — Microsoft, Google, Amazon. All three are now signed and public. Nothing CONCRETE or SCHEDULED follows; the only things ahead are speculative ('will more companies follow?', 'when will fuel contracts be inked?'). That is not a pending catalyst — it is a question mark. (b) Mainstream saturation confirmed: multiple outlets running 'stocks to watch' listicles, Motley Fool's 'Is It Too Late?' framing on OKLO, InvestorPlace calling it 'the smartest AI play', David Tepper publicly arguing the other side — the debate has gone fully retail. Week 1 already flagged heavy saturation from day one; Week 2 confirms it has deepened, not faded. The structural uranium demand story is real and durable, but the event-driven early-edge window — which is what this position is built on — is closed. Conviction falls to 2 (cull floor). Exit.
2024-10-25.exit_advice: Exit all positions. Third Exit fires on both prongs: (a) arc complete — Microsoft/TMC, Google/Kairos, and Amazon/X-energy are all signed; no new Big Tech nuclear signing is scheduled or even formally announced, only speculation; (b) crowd fully arrived — Yahoo Finance and Business Insider both run '7 nuclear stocks to watch' listicles; Motley Fool publishes 'Is It Too Late to Buy?' on OKLO (already doubled); InvestorPlace, Forbes, ETFTrends all piling in within the same week. The early-entry edge that justified the position is fully spent.

2025-01-24.thesis: NASA awards Intuitive Machines major lunar services contract Jan 2025
2025-01-24.exit_case: Named NASA contract award publicly occurred today and is actively being priced into LUNR.
2025-01-24.assessment: NASA's major lunar services contract to Intuitive Machines landed Jan 23-24, 2025, with LUNR skyrocketing on the news. Treating this as a STRUCTURAL catalyst: the award is an IDIQ framework (reportedly ~$4.82B, 10-year Near Space Network services vehicle) where the named award is only the first milestone — individual task orders, mission assignments, and program expansions are the concrete forward catalysts still to land over a decade. The devil's-advocate case (the award itself has already fired) does NOT resolve the thesis because no task order revenue or mission milestone has yet occurred; the IDIQ structure keeps catalyst_resolved false. Coverage is early-stage — one Motley Fool piece, no mainstream sell-side saturation yet — so the edge remains intact. LUNR is the direct pure-play. RDW (space infrastructure beneficiary of expanded NASA lunar spend) and RKLB (launch/spacecraft components for lunar missions) are listed known peers; both retain plausible exposure to the broader NASA lunar buildout this contract anchors. Conviction 7: named, specific, still-early catalyst with clear scheduled milestones ahead, tempered only by the immediate pricing spike on entry day.
2025-01-24.exit_advice: Hold through silence; exit only on a genuine reversal — NASA cancels or materially cuts the lunar program, or LUNR is stripped of follow-on task orders under the IDIQ framework.

LUNR remains the primary pure-play. RKLB retains plausible lunar-launch exposure. RDW remains a secondary space-infrastructure beneficiary. All three are held pending the next concrete task-order announcement.

2025-03-21.exit_case: The March 18 Bundestag vote — the named catalyst — has already occurred and is backward-looking.
2025-03-21.assessment: First-week entry. The March 18 Bundestag vote is the ignition point — not the endpoint — of a structural multi-year German defence buildout. The rules explicitly list 'European rearmament → debt-brake vote → budget votes → contracts → further summits' as the archetypal structural driver, and this event maps exactly onto that template. The debt-brake exemption is now constitutional law, creating an enduring spending regime that will generate fresh concrete milestones (federal budget implementation, Bundeswehr modernisation tenders, EU-level coordination, bilateral procurement agreements). We entered three days after the vote precisely because the structural thesis was just beginning. The single substantive article (investing.com, March 18) calls Rheinmetall 'still under-owned' and the buildout 'historic' and 'multi-year' — confirming the thesis is live and not yet mainstream-saturated. The rest of this week's press is generic 'stocks to watch' filler with no thesis signal. RNMBY is the clearest pure-play (Europe's largest ammunition maker, named frontline winner). BAESY, SAABY, THLLY, and FINMY all benefit from the European rearmament wave catalysed by Germany's commitment. Conviction 8: highly specific datable trigger, clear structural regime shift, credible under-owned vehicles. Withheld from 9-10 because the vote itself received mainstream press at inception.
2025-03-21.exit_advice: Hold. This is a structural, open-ended driver: the debt-brake exemption inaugurates years of procurement cycles, budget allocations, and contract awards — each a fresh concrete milestone. Exit only on genuine reversal of the driver itself (legislative rollback of the exemption, a peace deal ending the rearmament rationale, or a sharp funding cut). Do not exit on silence between milestones.

2025-03-28.thesis: NASA awards Intuitive Machines major lunar services contract Jan 2025
2025-03-28.exit_case: IM-2 executed, next task-order or IM-3 milestone unscheduled; seven weeks cumulative silence drain any remaining early-edge premium.
2025-03-28.assessment: This week's news is entirely irrelevant — a generic market-movement piece and a SpaceX-China investment controversy, neither touching Intuitive Machines, the NASA lunar services IDIQ, or any known vehicle. Zero fresh coverage of the catalyst for the second consecutive week post-IM-2. Per the silence-decay rule, conviction steps down one further notch (3→2), returning to the mechanical cull floor last seen in late February before IM-2 temporarily reset it. The structural thesis is unchanged: the 10-year IDIQ framework has not been cancelled, reversed, or mainstream-saturated; future task orders and mission assignments remain theoretically pending over the decade-long window. But IM-2 is now well behind us (executed imperfectly, persistent post-mission stock weakness), and no concrete next milestone — new task-order award, IM-3 announcement, contract expansion — has been scheduled or announced. The devil's-advocate case (nothing concrete ahead, seven cumulative weeks of silence) does not fire a hard structural exit absent a counter-event, but it fully consumes whatever early-edge premium justified holding through the prior silence stretches. All three vehicles retain their original exposure logic — LUNR as direct pure-play, RKLB for launch/spacecraft components on future lunar missions, RDW as space-infrastructure beneficiary — but without a fresh milestone this basket is functionally inert at the culling threshold. A single concrete task-order or mission announcement would reset conviction; absent that, this position exits the shortlist by its own score.
2025-03-28.exit_advice: Structurally alive (IDIQ not cancelled/reversed, no counter-event), but conviction has decayed to 2 for the second time — effectively culled by silence. Hold in name only pending any concrete task-order award or IM-3 mission announcement; absent that, the position is mechanically out-competed on the shortlist.

2025-04-04.thesis: NASA awards Intuitive Machines major lunar services contract Jan 2025
2025-04-04.exit_case: IM-2 executed; no task order or IM-3 scheduled; only speculative upside remains, nothing concrete ahead.
2025-04-04.assessment: The only item touching this catalyst this week is the Seeking Alpha 'SA Asks: Which space stocks are attractive right now?' piece (Apr 4) that names LUNR. It constitutes fresh coverage of this specific ticker, which halts the silence-decay clock for one week — conviction stays at 2 rather than falling to 1. However, the piece is a generic retail-discussion prompt, not a concrete new milestone (no task-order award, no IM-3 announcement, no contract expansion), so no conviction upgrade is warranted. The other three articles (telecom screener, crude-oil/PMI, tariff sensitivity) are entirely irrelevant. The structural thesis is unchanged: the 10-year IDIQ framework has not been cancelled, reversed, or mainstream-saturated; individual task orders and future missions remain theoretically pending. But IM-2 executed imperfectly nearly a month ago, post-mission stock weakness has persisted, and nothing concrete is scheduled or announced ahead — only speculative hope for a next milestone. Three consecutive post-IM-2 weeks of effective silence have fully consumed the early-edge premium. The broader tariff macro environment (CNBC) could weigh on small-cap space names generally, adding a further headwind, though it is not specific to this catalyst. All three vehicles retain their original exposure logic — LUNR as direct pure-play, RKLB for launch/spacecraft components on future lunar missions, RDW as space-infrastructure beneficiary of the broader NASA lunar buildout — but without a fresh milestone this basket is functionally inert and exits the shortlist by its own score.
2025-04-04.exit_advice: Hold on structural grounds only — the IDIQ has not been cancelled or reversed, so no hard exit fires. But conviction sits at the mechanical cull floor for a second straight week; this position should lose the shortlist fitness contest absent a concrete task-order award or IM-3 announcement. A single such announcement resets conviction immediately.

2025-09-12.thesis: SpaceX $17B spectrum license acquisition from EchoStar pending regulatory close
2025-09-12.exit_case: Deal terms ($17B) are fully public and priced into SATS at announcement; remaining edge is thin regulatory-close premium only.
2025-09-12.assessment: Week 1. SpaceX's $17B spectrum license acquisition from EchoStar was publicly announced Sept 9–10, 2025, per Baystreet.ca and Biztoc coverage. The CATALYST entered on is the pending regulatory close — that event has NOT yet occurred. This is a clean M&A pending-close setup: SATS (EchoStar) is the direct pure-play as the seller, receiving the $17B in value contingent on regulatory sign-off. Coverage so far is specialized (space/telecom sector press), not yet mainstream saturated. Telecom peers (TMUS etc.) sold off on the news, confirming SpaceX as a disruptive entrant — reinforcing the deal's strategic significance. No regulatory timeline has been published yet; process could span months. Devil's advocate: the announcement is public and SATS has likely repriced toward deal value, so the remaining event-driven edge is the regulatory-close spread, not a discovery play. Conviction held at 7: the catalyst is specific, named, $17B in scale, still in early press cycle, with a clear pure-play vehicle.
2025-09-12.exit_advice: Hold SATS until FCC/regulatory close is confirmed or the deal collapses. Exit triggers: (1) regulatory approval granted and deal formally closes, (2) deal terminated or blocked by regulators. Do not exit on quiet stretches during regulatory review.

2025-11-07.thesis: Germany's Bundestag March 18 vote exempts defence from debt brake
2025-11-07.exit_case: 34 weeks of unbroken silence; original ignition vote fully absorbed; no concrete milestone headline ever produced across the entire journal.
2025-11-07.assessment: Week 34 of complete thesis silence — the longest unbroken stretch since entry. No fresh coverage of German defence spending, Bundeswehr procurement, EU rearmament coordination, or any of the five vehicles has appeared this week or in any week since entry week one. Silence-decay cannot push conviction below the floor of 1, so conviction holds at 1 for the twenty-sixth consecutive week. Devil's advocate at its strongest: 34 weeks — nearly eight-and-a-half months — of unbroken silence is an extraordinary compounding fade; the original ignition vote is fully absorbed; the repeatedly cited pending milestones (federal defence-budget implementation vote, Bundeswehr modernisation tenders, EU procurement coordination) have not produced a single concrete scheduled headline across the entire 34-week journal, and the case for treating them as speculative rather than formally expected has never been stronger. Counter-argument sustained per structural-driver rules: the March 18 vote was designated the ignition of a multi-year regime shift — not a closed binary — and the rules require a concrete counter-event (ceasefire, policy reversal, funding cut) to resolve a structural driver; none has occurred. The constitutional debt-brake exemption remains embedded in law, the Ukraine conflict continues, and no peace deal or defence-budget reversal has materialised. The third-exit 'arc complete AND mainstream-saturated' test still does not fire: coverage is absent (not mainstream-loud). All five vehicles retain their structural rationale — RNMBY the clearest pure-play as Europe's largest ammunition maker, BAESY, SAABY, THLLY, and FINMY as the rearmament-peer basket — and none has suffered an own-thesis reversal. At conviction 1 this is a purely dormant structural hold that cannot compete against any event generating active press; it exits every shortlist through the fitness filter but is preserved intact for the reset scenario should fresh milestone coverage resume.
2025-11-07.exit_advice: Hold dormant. No concrete counter-event (ceasefire, policy reversal, funding cut) has reversed the structural driver. Constitutional debt-brake exemption remains embedded in law. Exit only on a genuine reversal of the driver itself, not on silence.

2025-11-07.assessment: Week 9 of unbroken silence on the SpaceX/EchoStar $17B spectrum acquisition. No FCC filing, no regulatory timeline, no deal opposition, no amendment, no withdrawal, and no close has appeared across the entire nine-week monitoring window. The soft cull is maintained (thesis_live=true, catalyst_resolved=false) solely because the closing event is still formally pending. SATS remains the sole direct pure-play but carries zero practical allocation weight.

2025-11-21.thesis: Germany's Bundestag March 18 vote exempts defence from debt brake
2025-11-21.exit_case: U.S.-led Ukraine peace push sent European defence stocks (Rheinmetall named) down 3%+; ceasefire progress could reverse rearmament urgency.
2025-11-21.assessment: Week 36 — the 35-week silence breaks. Investing.com (2025-11-19) is the first thesis-relevant article since entry week one: it directly names Rheinmetall (RNMBY), reports a 3%+ sector sell-off in European aerospace and defence, and ties the move explicitly to 'a fresh U.S.-led push to end the Russia-Ukraine war.' Silence-decay conviction resets upward from the floor of 1. This is unambiguously on-thesis coverage. However, this week's news also introduces the strongest counter-pressure seen since entry: if a U.S.-brokered Ukraine peace process produces a concrete ceasefire or peace deal, European rearmament urgency would diminish materially — that is precisely the 'concrete counter-event' that would force exit under the structural-driver rules. The distinction matters: 'signs of a push' is a directional signal, not a signed agreement. The structural driver remains intact — Germany's constitutional debt-brake exemption for defence is law, the Bundeswehr modernisation programme continues, and NATO burden-sharing concerns (the original second leg of the thesis) are independent of any Ukraine resolution. The third-exit 'arc complete AND mainstream-saturated' test still does not fire: fresh on-thesis coverage is the opposite of mainstream saturation, and at least one concrete future catalyst (any binding Ukraine peace framework, or formal EU defence-budget vote) remains possible. Conviction resets to 5: fresh direct coverage with a named vehicle lifts from the floor, but the peace-deal headwind, 9 months of thesis aging since the original vote, and a 3%+ adverse market reaction prevent a full reset to prior levels. All five vehicles retain structural rationale; RNMBY is the clearest pure-play and is explicitly named in this week's coverage.
2025-11-21.exit_advice: Hold, but elevate monitoring. No ceasefire signed and no concrete peace framework yet — 'signs of a U.S. push' is speculative, not a binding counter-event. Germany's constitutional debt-brake exemption remains law and Bundeswehr modernisation is ongoing. EXIT trigger: a signed Ukraine ceasefire or peace agreement, or a formal German/EU decision to reverse or cap the defence-spending trajectory. Watch closely — this week's news is the first credible directional headwind in 36 weeks.

2025-11-28.thesis: Germany's Bundestag March 18 vote exempts defence from debt brake
2025-11-28.exit_case: U.S.-brokered Ukraine peace process, if it produces a signed ceasefire, directly reverses European rearmament urgency — the structural driver's core premise.
2025-11-28.assessment: Week 37. Last week's silence break (investing.com naming RNMBY, sector sell-off tied to U.S. peace push) reset conviction to 5. This week returns to complete thesis silence — no fresh coverage of German defence spending, Bundeswehr procurement, EU rearmament, or any of the five vehicles. Silence-decay applies: conviction steps down one notch from 5 to 4. Devil's advocate (strongest case): the U.S.-led Ukraine peace initiative reported last week is the most credible threat to this structural thesis yet — if it crystallises into a signed ceasefire, the rearmament urgency underpinning the European defence build-up diminishes materially, and that is precisely the 'concrete counter-event' required for exit under structural-driver rules. Counter-argument: a diplomatic 'push' is not a signed deal; the constitutional debt-brake exemption remains embedded in German law; the Bundeswehr modernisation programme continues; and NATO burden-sharing concerns are independent of any Ukraine outcome. The third-exit test does not fire: last week's coverage was a sector sell-off note, not mainstream-saturated bullishness, and no arc-complete condition exists. All five vehicles retain structural rationale. RNMBY remains the clearest pure-play (named explicitly in last week's on-thesis coverage). At conviction 4 this is a live but fading structural hold — it competes weakly against events generating active press, but is preserved for re-acceleration if a fresh concrete milestone (EU defence-budget vote, major Bundeswehr contract, NATO commitment) produces another silence break.
2025-11-28.exit_advice: Exit ONLY on a concrete, signed Ukraine ceasefire/peace framework or a formal German defence-budget reversal/funding cut. Do NOT exit on continued silence or the ongoing peace 'push' (a diplomatic process is not a signed deal). Hold until one of those hard events occurs.

LEU is the only clean pure-play on this specific catalyst. Conviction 7: catalyst is specific and datable, vehicle is the named company, still early/under-the-radar per Citron framing, with concrete follow-on milestones (contracts, funding) structurally ahead.

Counter-argument sustained per structural-driver rules: no ceasefire has been signed; Germany's constitutional debt-brake exemption for defence remains embedded in law; the Bundeswehr modernisation programme continues as a statutory commitment; NATO burden-sharing concerns are independent of any Ukraine resolution; and the third-exit 'arc complete AND mainstream-saturated' test does not fire (coverage is absent, not mainstream-loud). All five vehicles retain structural rationale — RNMBY the clearest pure-play as Europe's largest ammunition maker, explicitly named in the only recent on-thesis coverage; BAESY, SAABY, THLLY, and FINMY as the rearmament-peer basket — and none has suffered an own-thesis reversal. At conviction 2 this is a deeply fading dormant structural hold, preserved intact solely for the reset scenario should a fresh concrete milestone produce another silence break.
2025-12-12.exit_advice: Hold dormant. Exit ONLY on a signed Ukraine ceasefire/peace framework, a German constitutional reversal of the debt-brake exemption, or a formal funding cut to the Bundeswehr modernisation programme. Reset upward if a fresh concrete milestone (EU defence-budget vote, major Bundeswehr contract award, NATO burden-sharing commitment) produces on-thesis coverage naming a basket vehicle.
